revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 御井神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|250px|御井神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 島根県簸川郡斐川町直江町2518 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|22|47.5|N|132|49|41|E|}} |祭神 = 木俣神 |社格 = 式内社
郷社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = |例祭 = 10月9日 |神事 = }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[簸川郡]][[斐川町]]にある神社である。[[大国主の神話]]に登場する八上比売（やがみひめ）とその子である[[木俣神]]（このまたのかみ）にまつわる伝承のある神社で、安産の神として信仰されている。 == 祭神 == *木俣神（御井神） 『[[古事記]]』に描かれる大国主の神話に登場する。[[大国主|大穴牟遅神]]と結婚した八上比売は、子を身ごもり[[出雲国]]まで来たが、正妻の[[スセリビメ|須勢理毘売]]を畏れ、生まれた子を木の俣に押し込んで[[因幡国]]に帰ってしまった。そのためにその子は木俣神と名づけられたとのことである。 == 歴史 == 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[出雲郡]]条に記載された在神祇官社の御井社、[[延喜式神名帳]]出雲国出雲郡に記載された御井神社に比定される。社地の近くにはかつての[[大社造]]の社殿の遺構と考えられる9本柱の柱穴がある杉沢III遺跡が発掘されており、初期の社地と推定されている。 == 摂末社 == *天神社 *実巽神社（じっそんじんじゃ） - 境外社、母神の八上比売を祀る == 信仰 == 神社の近くに「生井（いくい）」、「福井（さくい）」、「綱長井（つながい）」と呼ばれる3つの[[井戸]]があり、八上比売が出産の時に産湯を使ったという伝承がある。元来この3つの井戸に対する信仰が神社に発展したものと考えられる。 ==参考文献== *白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』　1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:斐川町]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 御井神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|250px|御井神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 島根県簸川郡斐川町直江町2518 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|22|47.5|N|132|49|41|E|}} |祭神 = 木俣神 |社格 = 式内社
郷社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = |例祭 = 10月9日 |神事 = }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[簸川郡]][[斐川町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[大国主の神話]]に登場する八上比売（やがみひめ）とその子である[[木俣神]]（このまたのかみ）にまつわる伝承のある神社で、安産の神として信仰されている。 == 祭神 == * 木俣神（御井神） 『[[古事記]]』に描かれる大国主の[[神話]]に登場する。[[大国主|大穴牟遅神]]と結婚した八上比売は、子を身ごもり[[出雲国]]まで来たが、正妻の[[スセリビメ|須勢理毘売]]を畏れ、生まれた子を木の俣に押し込んで[[因幡国]]に帰ってしまった。そのためにその子は木俣神と名づけられたとのことである。 == 歴史 == 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[出雲郡]]条に記載された在神祇官社の御井社、[[延喜式神名帳]]出雲国出雲郡に記載された御井神社に比定される。社地の近くにはかつての[[大社造]]の社殿の遺構と考えられる9本柱の柱穴がある杉沢III遺跡が発掘されており、初期の社地と推定されている。 == 摂末社 == * 天神社 * 実巽神社（じっそんじんじゃ） - 境外社、母神の八上比売を祀る == 信仰 == 神社の近くに「生井（いくい）」、「福井（さくい）」、「綱長井（つながい）」と呼ばれる3つの[[井戸]]があり、八上比売が出産の時に産湯を使ったという伝承がある。元来この3つの井戸に対する信仰が神社に発展したものと考えられる。 == 参考文献 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』 1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X * 関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * 瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:斐川町]]

郷社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = |例祭 = 10月9日 |神事 = }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[簸川郡]][[斐川町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[大国主の神話]]に登場する八上比売（やがみひめ）とその子である[[木俣神]]（このまたのかみ）にまつわる伝承のある神社で、安産の神として信仰されている。 == 祭神 == * 木俣神（御井神） 『[[古事記]]』に描かれる大国主の[[神話]]に登場する。[[大国主|大穴牟遅神]]と結婚した八上比売は、子を身ごもり[[出雲国]]まで来たが、正妻の[[スセリビメ|須勢理毘売]]を畏れ、生まれた子を木の俣に押し込んで[[因幡国]]に帰ってしまった。そのためにその子は木俣神と名づけられたとのことである。 == 歴史 == 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[出雲郡]]条に記載された在神祇官社の御井社、[[延喜式神名帳]]出雲国出雲郡に記載された御井神社に比定される。社地の近くにはかつての[[大社造]]の社殿の遺構と考えられる9本柱の柱穴がある杉沢III遺跡が発掘されており、初期の社地と推定されている。 == 摂末社 == * 天神社 * 実巽神社（じっそんじんじゃ） - 境外社、母神の八上比売を祀る == 信仰 == 神社の近くに「生井（いくい）」、「福井（さくい）」、「綱長井（つながい）」と呼ばれる3つの[[井戸]]があり、八上比売が出産の時に産湯を使ったという伝承がある。元来この3つの井戸に対する信仰が神社に発展したものと考えられる。 == 参考文献 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』 1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X * 関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * 瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:斐川町]]

{{神社 |名称 = 御井神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|250px|御井神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 島根県出雲市斐川町直江2518 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|22|47.5|N|132|49|41|E|}} |祭神 = 木俣神 |社格 = 式内社
郷社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = |例祭 = 10月9日 |神事 = }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[出雲市]]斐川町直江にある[[神社]]である。[[大国主の神話]]に登場する八上比売（やがみひめ）とその子である[[木俣神]]（このまたのかみ）にまつわる伝承のある神社で、安産の神として信仰されている。 == 祭神 == * 木俣神（御井神） 『[[古事記]]』に描かれる大国主の[[神話]]に登場する。[[大国主|大穴牟遅神]]と結婚した八上比売は、子を身ごもり[[出雲国]]まで来たが、正妻の[[スセリビメ|須勢理毘売]]を畏れ、生まれた子を木の俣に押し込んで[[因幡国]]に帰ってしまった。そのためにその子は木俣神と名づけられたとのことである。 == 歴史 == 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[出雲郡]]条に記載された在神祇官社の御井社、[[延喜式神名帳]]出雲国出雲郡に記載された御井神社に比定される。社地の近くにはかつての[[大社造]]の社殿の遺構と考えられる9本柱の柱穴がある杉沢III遺跡が発掘されており、初期の社地と推定されている。 == 摂末社 == * 天神社 * 実巽神社（じっそんじんじゃ） - 境外社、母神の八上比売を祀る == 信仰 == 神社の近くに「生井（いくい）」、「福井（さくい）」、「綱長井（つながい）」と呼ばれる3つの[[井戸]]があり、八上比売が出産の時に産湯を使ったという伝承がある。元来この3つの井戸に対する信仰が神社に発展したものと考えられる。 == 参考文献 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』 1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X * 関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * 瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]]

旧[[郷社]] |創建 = |本殿 = [[大社造]] |別名 = |例祭 = 10月9日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=御井神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[出雲市]]斐川町直江にある[[神社]]である。[[大国主の神話]]に登場する八上比売（やがみひめ）とその子である[[木俣神]]（このまたのかみ）にまつわる伝承のある神社で、安産の神として信仰されている。 == 祭神 == * 木俣神（御井神） 『[[古事記]]』に描かれる大国主の[[神話]]に登場する。[[大国主|大穴牟遅神]]と結婚した八上比売は、子を身ごもり[[出雲国]]まで来たが、正妻の[[スセリビメ|須勢理毘売]]を畏れ、生まれた子を木の俣に押し込んで[[因幡国]]に帰ってしまった。そのためにその子は木俣神と名づけられたとのことである。 == 歴史 == 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[出雲郡]]条に記載された在神祇官社の御井社、[[延喜式神名帳]]出雲国出雲郡に記載された御井神社に比定される。社地の近くにはかつての[[大社造]]の社殿の遺構と考えられる9本柱の柱穴がある杉沢III遺跡が発掘されており、初期の社地と推定されている。 == 摂末社 == * 天神社 * 実巽神社（じっそんじんじゃ） - 境外社、母神の八上比売を祀る == 信仰 == 神社の近くに「生井（いくい）」、「福井（さくい）」、「綱長井（つながい）」と呼ばれる3つの[[井戸]]があり、八上比売が出産の時に産湯を使ったという伝承がある。元来この3つの井戸に対する信仰が神社に発展したものと考えられる。 == 参考文献 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』 1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X * 関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * 瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Hikawami Shrine'''|御井神社 (斐川町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

* {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|de|Shintō-Schrein|zh|神社|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|ru|Дзиндзя|WD=Q845945}}

* {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342}}

* {{ill|Kokuhei Shosha (Engishiki)|WD=Q135206465}}
* {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|WD=Q135026601}}

{{nihongo|'''Hikawami Shrine'''|御井神社 (斐川町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Hikawami Shrine'''|御井神社 (斐川町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Hikawami Shrine'''|御井神社 (斐川町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Hikawami Shrine'''|御井神社 (斐川町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Hikawami Shrine'''|御井神社 (斐川町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Hikawami Shrine'''|御井神社 (斐川町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Hikawami Shrine'''|御井神社 (斐川町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Hikawami Shrine'''|御井神社 (斐川町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 御井神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|270px|御井神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[島根県]][[出雲市]]斐川町直江2518 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 木俣神 |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = |本殿 = [[大社造]] |別名 = |例祭 = 10月9日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=御井神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[出雲市]]斐川町直江にある[[神社]]である。[[大国主の神話]]に登場する八上比売（やがみひめ）とその子である[[木俣神]]（このまたのかみ）にまつわる伝承のある神社で、安産の神として信仰されている。 == 祭神 == * 木俣神（御井神） 『[[古事記]]』に描かれる大国主の[[神話]]に登場する。[[大国主|大穴牟遅神]]と結婚した八上比売は、子を身ごもり[[出雲国]]まで来たが、正妻の[[スセリビメ|須勢理毘売]]を畏れ、生まれた子を木の俣に押し込んで[[因幡国]]に帰ってしまった。そのためにその子は木俣神と名づけられたとのことである。 == 歴史 == 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[出雲郡]]条に記載された在神祇官社の御井社、[[延喜式神名帳]]出雲国出雲郡に記載された御井神社に比定される。社地の近くにはかつての[[大社造]]の社殿の遺構と考えられる9本柱の柱穴がある杉沢III遺跡が発掘されており、初期の社地と推定されている。 == 摂末社 == * 天神社 * 実巽神社（じっそんじんじゃ） - 境外社、母神の八上比売を祀る == 信仰 == 神社の近くに「生井（いくい）」、「福井（さくい）」、「綱長井（つながい）」と呼ばれる3つの[[井戸]]があり、八上比売が出産の時に産湯を使ったという伝承がある。元来この3つの井戸に対する信仰が神社に発展したものと考えられる。 == 参考文献 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』 1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X * 関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * 瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[御井神社 (出雲市)]] to [[Hikawami Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Merging Japanese Wikipedia import with English content

{{神社 |名称 = 御井神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|270px|御井神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[島根県]][[出雲市]]斐川町直江2518 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 木俣神 |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = |本殿 = [[大社造]] |別名 = |例祭 = 10月9日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=御井神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[出雲市]]斐川町直江にある[[神社]]である。[[大国主の神話]]に登場する八上比売（やがみひめ）とその子である[[木俣神]]（このまたのかみ）にまつわる伝承のある神社で、安産の神として信仰されている。 == 祭神 == * 木俣神（御井神） 『[[古事記]]』に描かれる大国主の[[神話]]に登場する。[[大国主|大穴牟遅神]]と結婚した八上比売は、子を身ごもり[[出雲国]]まで来たが、正妻の[[スセリビメ|須勢理毘売]]を畏れ、生まれた子を木の俣に押し込んで[[因幡国]]に帰ってしまった。そのためにその子は木俣神と名づけられたとのことである。 == 歴史 == 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[出雲郡]]条に記載された在神祇官社の御井社、[[延喜式神名帳]]出雲国出雲郡に記載された御井神社に比定される。社地の近くにはかつての[[大社造]]の社殿の遺構と考えられる9本柱の柱穴がある杉沢III遺跡が発掘されており、初期の社地と推定されている。 == 摂末社 == * 天神社 * 実巽神社（じっそんじんじゃ） - 境外社、母神の八上比売を祀る == 信仰 == 神社の近くに「生井（いくい）」、「福井（さくい）」、「綱長井（つながい）」と呼ばれる3つの[[井戸]]があり、八上比売が出産の時に産湯を使ったという伝承がある。元来この3つの井戸に対する信仰が神社に発展したものと考えられる。 == 参考文献 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』 1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X * 関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * 瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]]

Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[JA:御井神社 (出雲市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488772}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|御井神社 (出雲市)|version=107339316|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 御井神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|270px|御井神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[島根県]][[出雲市]]斐川町直江2518 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 木俣神 |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = |本殿 = [[大社造]] |別名 = |例祭 = 10月9日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=御井神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[出雲市]]斐川町直江にある[[神社]]である。[[大国主の神話]]に登場する八上比売（やがみひめ）とその子である[[木俣神]]（このまたのかみ）にまつわる伝承のある神社で、安産の神として信仰されている。 == 祭神 == * 木俣神（御井神） 『[[古事記]]』に描かれる大国主の[[神話]]に登場する。[[大国主|大穴牟遅神]]と結婚した八上比売は、子を身ごもり[[出雲国]]まで来たが、正妻の[[スセリビメ|須勢理毘売]]を畏れ、生まれた子を木の俣に押し込んで[[因幡国]]に帰ってしまった。そのためにその子は木俣神と名づけられたとのことである。 == 歴史 == 『[[出雲国風土記]]』[[出雲郡]]条に記載された在神祇官社の御井社、[[延喜式神名帳]]出雲国出雲郡に記載された御井神社に比定される。社地の近くにはかつての[[大社造]]の社殿の遺構と考えられる9本柱の柱穴がある杉沢III遺跡が発掘されており、初期の社地と推定されている。 == 摂末社 == * 天神社 * 実巽神社（じっそんじんじゃ） - 境外社、母神の八上比売を祀る == 信仰 == 神社の近くに「生井（いくい）」、「福井（さくい）」、「綱長井（つながい）」と呼ばれる3つの[[井戸]]があり、八上比売が出産の時に産湯を使ったという伝承がある。元来この3つの井戸に対する信仰が神社に発展したものと考えられる。 == 参考文献 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』 1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X * 関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * 瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]]

旧{{jalink|郷社}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|大社造|Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = 10月9日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=御井神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は{{jalink|島根県|Q132751}}{{jalink|出雲市|Q825166}}斐川町直江にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|大国主の神話|Q11433656}}に登場する八上比売（やがみひめ）とその子である{{jalink|木俣神|Q11092833}}（このまたのかみ）にまつわる伝承のある神社で、安産の神として信仰されている。 == 祭神 == * 木俣神（御井神） 『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』に描かれる大国主の{{jalink|神話|Q12827256}}に登場する。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大穴牟遅神}}と結婚した八上比売は、子を身ごもり{{jalink|出雲国|Q582846}}まで来たが、正妻の{{jalink|スセリビメ|Q8191715|須勢理毘売}}を畏れ、生まれた子を木の俣に押し込んで{{jalink|因幡国|Q1054338}}に帰ってしまった。そのためにその子は木俣神と名づけられたとのことである。 == 歴史 == 『{{jalink|出雲国風土記|Q11395858}}』{{jalink|出雲郡|Q10896679}}条に記載された在神祇官社の御井社、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}出雲国出雲郡に記載された御井神社に比定される。社地の近くにはかつての{{jalink|大社造|Q387874}}の社殿の遺構と考えられる9本柱の柱穴がある杉沢III遺跡が発掘されており、初期の社地と推定されている。 == 摂末社 == * 天神社 * 実巽神社（じっそんじんじゃ） - 境外社、母神の八上比売を祀る == 信仰 == 神社の近くに「生井（いくい）」、「福井（さくい）」、「綱長井（つながい）」と呼ばれる3つの{{jalink|井戸|Q43483}}があり、八上比売が出産の時に産湯を使ったという伝承がある。元来この3つの井戸に対する信仰が神社に発展したものと考えられる。 == 参考文献 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』 1997年 {{jalink|東京堂出版|Q11524645}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * 関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 {{jalink|明石書店|Q11512528}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * 瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 {{jalink|新人物往来社|Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]]

旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|大社造|Q387874}} |別名 = |例祭 = 10月9日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=御井神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は{{jalink|島根県|Q132751}}{{jalink|出雲市|Q825166}}斐川町直江にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|大国主の神話|Q11433656}}に登場する八上比売（やがみひめ）とその子である{{jalink|木俣神|Q11092833}}（このまたのかみ）にまつわる伝承のある神社で、安産の神として信仰されている。 === 祭神 === * 木俣神（御井神） 『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』に描かれる大国主の{{jalink|神話|Q12827256}}に登場する。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大穴牟遅神}}と結婚した八上比売は、子を身ごもり{{jalink|出雲国|Q582846}}まで来たが、正妻の{{jalink|スセリビメ|Q8191715|須勢理毘売}}を畏れ、生まれた子を木の俣に押し込んで{{jalink|因幡国|Q1054338}}に帰ってしまった。そのためにその子は木俣神と名づけられたとのことである。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国風土記|Q11395858}}』{{jalink|出雲郡|Q10896679}}条に記載された在神祇官社の御井社、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}出雲国出雲郡に記載された御井神社に比定される。社地の近くにはかつての{{jalink|大社造|Q387874}}の社殿の遺構と考えられる9本柱の柱穴がある杉沢III遺跡が発掘されており、初期の社地と推定されている。 === 摂末社 === * 天神社 * 実巽神社（じっそんじんじゃ） - 境外社、母神の八上比売を祀る === 信仰 === 神社の近くに「生井（いくい）」、「福井（さくい）」、「綱長井（つながい）」と呼ばれる3つの{{jalink|井戸|Q43483}}があり、八上比売が出産の時に産湯を使ったという伝承がある。元来この3つの井戸に対する信仰が神社に発展したものと考えられる。 === 参考文献 === * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』 1997年 {{jalink|東京堂出版|Q11524645}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * 関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 {{jalink|明石書店|Q11512528}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * 瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 {{jalink|新人物往来社|Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3280005003117 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市斐川町直江2518', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '島根県出雲市斐川町直江町2518', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (出雲市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488772}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Mii Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden |Location = {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}} Hikawa-chō Naoe 2518 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Konamata-no-Kami |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} |Alternative name = |Annual festival = October 9 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Mii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hikawa-chō Naoe, {{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}. It is a shrine associated with traditions concerning Yagami-hime, who appears in the {{jalink|Ōkuninushi myths|Q11433656}}, and her child {{jalink|Konamata-no-Kami|Q11092833}} (this deity), and is worshipped as a god of safe childbirth. === Enshrined deity === * Konamata-no-Kami (Mii-no-Kami) He appears in the myths of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuchi-no-Kami}} depicted in the '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'. Yagami-hime, who married Ōkuninushi, became pregnant and came as far as {{jalink|Izumo Province|Q582846}}, but fearing the principal wife {{jalink|Suseri-hime|Q8191715|Suseri-bime}}, she pushed the newborn child into the fork of a tree and returned to {{jalink|Inaba Province|Q1054338}}. Because of this, the child was named Konamata-no-Kami. === History === The shrine is identified with the Mii Shrine listed as an existing government shrine (zaishingi-kan-sha) in the Izumo District entry of the '{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}}', and with the Mii Shrine recorded in the Izumo District of Izumo Province in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'. Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{jalink|wells|Q43483}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{jalink|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo no Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{jalink|Akashi Shoten|Q11512528}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{jalink|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mii Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11488772}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Mii Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden |Location = {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}} Hikawa-chō Naoe 2518 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Konamata-no-Kami |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} |Alternative name = |Annual festival = October 9 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Mii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hikawa-chō Naoe, {{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}. It is a shrine associated with traditions concerning Yagami-hime, who appears in the {{jalink|Ōkuninushi myths|Q11433656}}, and her child {{jalink|Konamata-no-Kami|Q11092833}} (this deity), and is worshipped as a god of safe childbirth. === Enshrined deity === * Konamata-no-Kami (Mii-no-Kami) He appears in the myths of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuchi-no-Kami}} depicted in the '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'. Yagami-hime, who married Ōkuninushi, became pregnant and came as far as {{jalink|Izumo Province|Q582846}}, but fearing the principal wife {{jalink|Suseri-hime|Q8191715|Suseri-bime}}, she pushed the newborn child into the fork of a tree and returned to {{jalink|Inaba Province|Q1054338}}. Because of this, the child was named Konamata-no-Kami. === History === The shrine is identified with the Mii Shrine listed as an existing government shrine (zaishingi-kan-sha) in the Izumo District entry of the '{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}}', and with the Mii Shrine recorded in the Izumo District of Izumo Province in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'. Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{jalink|wells|Q43483}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{jalink|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo no Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{jalink|Akashi Shoten|Q11512528}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{jalink|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mii Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Hikawa, Shimane|lt=Hikawa|en|Hikawa, Shimane|ja|斐川町|ko|히카와정 (시마네현)|ru|Хикава (Симане)|tl|Hikawa, Shimane|zh|斐川町|WD=Q1132034}} * Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q11092833|lt=Q11092833|WD=Q11092833}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182905 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|lt=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|lt=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3280005003117 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県出雲市斐川町直江2518', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '島根県出雲市斐川町直江町2518', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (出雲市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488772}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Mii Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden |Location = {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}} Hikawa-chō Naoe 2518 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Konamata-no-Kami |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} |Alternative name = |Annual festival = October 9 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Mii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hikawa-chō Naoe, {{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}, {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}. It is a shrine associated with traditions concerning Yagami-hime, who appears in the {{jalink|Ōkuninushi myths|Q11433656}}, and her child {{jalink|Konamata-no-Kami|Q11092833}} (this deity), and is worshipped as a god of safe childbirth. === Enshrined deity === * Konamata-no-Kami (Mii-no-Kami) He appears in the myths of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuchi-no-Kami}} depicted in the '{{jalink|Kojiki|Q813031}}'. Yagami-hime, who married Ōkuninushi, became pregnant and came as far as {{jalink|Izumo Province|Q582846}}, but fearing the principal wife {{jalink|Suseri-hime|Q8191715|Suseri-bime}}, she pushed the newborn child into the fork of a tree and returned to {{jalink|Inaba Province|Q1054338}}. Because of this, the child was named Konamata-no-Kami. === History === The shrine is identified with the Mii Shrine listed as an existing government shrine (zaishingi-kan-sha) in the Izumo District entry of the '{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}}', and with the Mii Shrine recorded in the Izumo District of Izumo Province in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'. Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{jalink|wells|Q43483}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{jalink|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo no Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{jalink|Akashi Shoten|Q11512528}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{jalink|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mii Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]]

Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Annual festival = October 9 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Mii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hikawa-chō Naoe, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a shrine associated with traditions concerning Yagami-hime, who appears in the {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi myths|Q11433656|WD=Q11433656|1=Myth of Ōkuninushi}}, and her child {{ill|lt=Konamata-no-Kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} (this deity), and is worshipped as a god of safe childbirth. === Enshrined deity === * Konamata-no-Kami (Mii-no-Kami) He appears in the myths of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} depicted in the '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'. Yagami-hime, who married Ōkuninushi, became pregnant and came as far as {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}, but fearing the principal wife {{ill|lt=Suseri-hime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suseri-bime|1=Suseri-Hime}}, she pushed the newborn child into the fork of a tree and returned to {{ill|lt=Inaba Province|Q1054338|WD=Q1054338|1=Inaba Province}}. Because of this, the child was named Konamata-no-Kami. === History === The shrine is identified with the Mii Shrine listed as an existing government shrine (zaishingi-kan-sha) in the Izumo District entry of the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}', and with the Mii Shrine recorded in the Izumo District of Izumo Province in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{ill|lt=wells|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mii Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{ill|lt=wells|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

== Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Mii Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} Hikawa-chō Naoe 2518 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Konamata-no-Kami |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Annual festival = October 9 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Mii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hikawa-chō Naoe, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a shrine associated with traditions concerning Yagami-hime, who appears in the {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi myths|Q11433656|WD=Q11433656|1=Myth of Ōkuninushi}}, and her child {{ill|lt=Konamata-no-Kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} (this deity), and is worshipped as a god of safe childbirth. === Enshrined deity === * Konamata-no-Kami (Mii-no-Kami) He appears in the myths of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} depicted in the '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'. Yagami-hime, who married Ōkuninushi, became pregnant and came as far as {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}, but fearing the principal wife {{ill|lt=Suseri-hime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suseri-bime|1=Suseri-Hime}}, she pushed the newborn child into the fork of a tree and returned to {{ill|lt=Inaba Province|Q1054338|WD=Q1054338|1=Inaba Province}}. Because of this, the child was named Konamata-no-Kami. === History === The shrine is identified with the Mii Shrine listed as an existing government shrine (zaishingi-kan-sha) in the Izumo District entry of the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}', and with the Mii Shrine recorded in the Izumo District of Izumo Province in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{ill|lt=wells|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Yagami-hime, who married Ōkuninushi, became pregnant and came as far as {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}, but fearing the principal wife {{ill|lt=Suseri-hime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suseri-bime|1=Suseri-Hime}}, she pushed the newborn child into the fork of a tree and returned to {{ill|lt=Inaba Province|Q1054338|WD=Q1054338|1=Inaba Province}}. Because of this, the child was named Konamata-no-Kami. === History === The shrine is identified with the Mii Shrine listed as an existing government shrine (zaishingi-kan-sha) in the Izumo District entry of the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}', and with the Mii Shrine recorded in the Izumo District of Izumo Province in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{ill|lt=wells|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Mii Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} Hikawa-chō Naoe 2518 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Konamata-no-Kami |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Annual festival = October 9 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Mii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hikawa-chō Naoe, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a shrine associated with traditions concerning Yagami-hime, who appears in the {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi myths|Q11433656|WD=Q11433656|1=Myth of Ōkuninushi}}, and her child {{ill|lt=Konamata-no-Kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} (this deity), and is worshipped as a god of safe childbirth. === Enshrined deity === * Konamata-no-Kami (Mii-no-Kami) He appears in the myths of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} depicted in the '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'. Yagami-hime, who married Ōkuninushi, became pregnant and came as far as {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}, but fearing the principal wife {{ill|lt=Suseri-hime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suseri-bime|1=Suseri-Hime}}, she pushed the newborn child into the fork of a tree and returned to {{ill|lt=Inaba Province|Q1054338|WD=Q1054338|1=Inaba Province}}. Because of this, the child was named Konamata-no-Kami. === History === The shrine is identified with the Mii Shrine listed as an existing government shrine (zaishingi-kan-sha) in the Izumo District entry of the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}', and with the Mii Shrine recorded in the Izumo District of Izumo Province in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{ill|lt=wells|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

It is a shrine associated with traditions concerning Yagami-hime, who appears in the {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi myths|Q11433656|WD=Q11433656|1=Myth of Ōkuninushi}}, and her child {{ill|lt=Konamata-no-Kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} (this deity), and is worshipped as a god of safe childbirth. === Enshrined deity === * Konamata-no-Kami (Mii-no-Kami) He appears in the myths of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} depicted in the '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'. Yagami-hime, who married Ōkuninushi, became pregnant and came as far as {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}, but fearing the principal wife {{ill|lt=Suseri-hime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suseri-bime|1=Suseri-Hime}}, she pushed the newborn child into the fork of a tree and returned to {{ill|lt=Inaba Province|Q1054338|WD=Q1054338|1=Inaba Province}}. Because of this, the child was named Konamata-no-Kami. === History === The shrine is identified with the Mii Shrine listed as an existing government shrine (zaishingi-kan-sha) in the Izumo District entry of the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}', and with the Mii Shrine recorded in the Izumo District of Izumo Province in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{ill|lt=wells|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{ill|lt=wells|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

"Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Annual festival = October 9 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Mii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hikawa-chō Naoe, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a shrine associated with traditions concerning Yagami-hime, who appears in the {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi myths|Q11433656|WD=Q11433656|1=Myth of Ōkuninushi}}, and her child {{ill|lt=Konamata-no-Kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} (this deity), and is worshipped as a god of safe childbirth. === Enshrined deity === * Konamata-no-Kami (Mii-no-Kami) He appears in the myths of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} depicted in the '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'. Yagami-hime, who married Ōkuninushi, became pregnant and came as far as {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}, but fearing the principal wife {{ill|lt=Suseri-hime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suseri-bime|1=Suseri-Hime}}, she pushed the newborn child into the fork of a tree and returned to {{ill|lt=Inaba Province|Q1054338|WD=Q1054338|1=Inaba Province}}. Because of this, the child was named Konamata-no-Kami. === History === The shrine is identified with the Mii Shrine listed as an existing government shrine (zaishingi-kan-sha) in the Izumo District entry of the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}', and with the Mii Shrine recorded in the Izumo District of Izumo Province in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{ill|lt=wells|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Mii Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} Hikawa-chō Naoe 2518 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Konamata-no-Kami |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Annual festival = October 9 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Mii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hikawa-chō Naoe, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a shrine associated with traditions concerning Yagami-hime, who appears in the {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi myths|Q11433656|WD=Q11433656|1=Myth of Ōkuninushi}}, and her child {{ill|lt=Konamata-no-Kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} (this deity), and is worshipped as a god of safe childbirth. === Enshrined deity === * Konamata-no-Kami (Mii-no-Kami) He appears in the myths of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} depicted in the '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'. Yagami-hime, who married Ōkuninushi, became pregnant and came as far as {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}, but fearing the principal wife {{ill|lt=Suseri-hime|Q8191715|WD=Q8191715|lt=Suseri-bime|1=Suseri-Hime}}, she pushed the newborn child into the fork of a tree and returned to {{ill|lt=Inaba Province|Q1054338|WD=Q1054338|1=Inaba Province}}. Because of this, the child was named Konamata-no-Kami. === History === The shrine is identified with the Mii Shrine listed as an existing government shrine (zaishingi-kan-sha) in the Izumo District entry of the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}', and with the Mii Shrine recorded in the Izumo District of Izumo Province in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{ill|lt=wells|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]]

== Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Hikawamiijinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Mii Shrine Haiden]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|qid=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} Hikawa-chō Naoe 2518 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Konamata-no-Kami |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|qid=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Annual festival = October 9 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Mii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hikawa-chō Naoe, {{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|qid=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a shrine associated with traditions concerning Yagami-hime, who appears in the {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi myths|Q11433656|qid=Q11433656|1=Myth of Ōkuninushi}}, and her child {{ill|lt=Konamata-no-Kami|Q11092833|qid=Q11092833|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} (this deity), and is worshipped as a god of safe childbirth. === Enshrined deity === * Konamata-no-Kami (Mii-no-Kami) He appears in the myths of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuchi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} depicted in the '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'. Yagami-hime, who married Ōkuninushi, became pregnant and came as far as {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|qid=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}, but fearing the principal wife {{ill|lt=Suseri-hime|Q8191715|qid=Q8191715|lt=Suseri-bime|1=Suseri-Hime}}, she pushed the newborn child into the fork of a tree and returned to {{ill|lt=Inaba Province|Q1054338|qid=Q1054338|1=Inaba Province}}. Because of this, the child was named Konamata-no-Kami. === History === The shrine is identified with the Mii Shrine listed as an existing government shrine (zaishingi-kan-sha) in the Izumo District entry of the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|qid=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}', and with the Mii Shrine recorded in the Izumo District of Izumo Province in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. Near the shrine grounds, the Sugisawa III site—where nine post holes believed to be the remains of an ancient {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|qid=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} shrine building were excavated—has been discovered, and is presumed to have been the original shrine site. === Subsidiary and branch shrines === * Tenjin Shrine * Jisson Shrine (じっそんじんじゃ) – an off-site shrine that enshrines the mother deity Yagami-hime === Faith === Near the shrine are three {{ill|lt=wells|Q43483|qid=Q43483|1=Well}} called “Ikui,” “Sakui,” and “Tsunagai,” and there is a tradition that Yagami-hime used their water for the birth bath at the time of childbirth. It is thought that worship of these three wells originally developed into the shrine. === References === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masanori Toki, *Newly Revised Popular Edition: Encyclopedia of Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|Q11524645|qid=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}} ISBN 4-490-10474-X * Kazuhiko Seki, *Annotated Studies on the “Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki”*, 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|qid=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Dictionary of Ancient Izumo*, 2001, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|qid=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine (Hikawa, Shimane)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]]
